creator: gerard_seibert@outlook.com
text:
Created attachment 246277
Log file of x11

uname: FreeBSD 13.2-RELEASE-p4 GENERIC amd64
freebsd-version: 13.2-RELEASE-p5

I am using “x11/budgie” as my desktop. Everything was working fine until I updated to FreeBSD-p5. After having poudriere update its jail, I had it update all of the ports on my system. Since then, I can no longer get “budgie” to run. A few seconds after I run “startx,” a warning appears on the screen that an error has occurred and recommends that I log out. If I don’t, a black screen appears with the icons for my installed apps. There is no taskbar or menu available. I can click on an icon, and it will run, but I cannot log out of the desktop. I have to manually shut down the machine and reboot it to regain control.

I tried rebuilding the x11/budgie port, but that did not help.

If someone wants to examine it, there is a huge “budgie-wm.core” and “budgie-daemon.core file. I have attached the Xorg.0.log file.
